title: mentha longifolia subsp. calliantha
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. calliantha, also known as wild marjoram, is a subspecies of the mint plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its upright stems and small, pale purple flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia subsp. calliantha include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe menstrual cramps, among others. This plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
